# Paragon Documentation

## Docs

- [ActionKit for Tool Calling](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/actionkit-for-tool-calling.md): Use ActionKit to provide your AI agent with hundreds of Integration Tools.
- [Logs and Errors](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/actionkit-logs.md): Search, view, and trace logs for all of your ActionKit requests and trigger events.
- [AI Agent Playground](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/actionkit-playground.md): Try ActionKit in the AI Agent Playground, a sandbox to test chatting with an agent that has access to ActionKit tools.
- [Tools API](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/api-reference.md): Give your AI agent or app access to thousands of tools from your users' integrations, from our catalog of pre-built Tools.
- [List Tools](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/api-reference/list-tools.md): List all Tools available for a user.
- [Run Tool](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/api-reference/run-tool.md): Run a Tool on behalf of your user.
- [Custom Tools](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/custom-tools.md): Use the Proxy API to create Custom Tools for any API or integration.
- [Getting Started](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/getting-started.md): Get started with the ActionKit API.
- [Create Folder](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/integrations/adobeexperiencemanager/ADOBE_EXPERIENCE_MANAGER_CREATE_FOLDER.md)

- [Adobe Experience Manager](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/integrations/adobeexperiencemanager/overview.md): Browse the tools available for Adobe Experience Manager in ActionKit.

- [Zoom](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/integrations/zoom/overview.md): Browse the tools and triggers available for Zoom in ActionKit.
- [New Meeting](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/integrations/zoom/triggers/ZOOM_TRIGGER_MEETING_CREATED.md): Trigger when a meeting is created in Zoom
- [Meeting Ended](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/integrations/zoom/triggers/ZOOM_TRIGGER_MEETING_ENDED.md): Trigger when a meeting host ends the meeting in Zoom
- [New Meeting Registrant](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/integrations/zoom/triggers/ZOOM_TRIGGER_MEETING_REGISTRANT_CREATED.md): Trigger when a user registers for a meeting in Zoom
- [Meeting Updated](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/integrations/zoom/triggers/ZOOM_TRIGGER_MEETING_UPDATED.md): Trigger when a meeting is updated in Zoom
- [Meeting Participant Joined](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/integrations/zoom/triggers/ZOOM_TRIGGER_PARTICIPANT_JOINED.md): Trigger when a participant joins a meeting in Zoom
- [Meeting Participant Left](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/integrations/zoom/triggers/ZOOM_TRIGGER_PARTICIPANT_LEFT.md): Trigger when a participant has left the meeting in Zoom
- [Complete Recording](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/integrations/zoom/triggers/ZOOM_TRIGGER_RECORDING_COMPLETED.md): Trigger when a meeting recording is ready to download
- [New Recording](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/integrations/zoom/triggers/ZOOM_TRIGGER_RECORDING_STARTED.md): Trigger when a meeting recording is started in Zoom
- [Recording Transcript Completed](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/integrations/zoom/triggers/ZOOM_TRIGGER_RECORDING_TRANSCRIPT_COMPLETED.md): Trigger when the transcript of the recording is made available
- [ActionKit](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/overview.md): ActionKit is an API to give your AI agent or app access to Paragon's catalog of pre-built Integration Tools and Triggers.
- [Schema Formats](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/schema-formats.md)
- [Supported Integrations](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/supported-integrations.md): See all the integrations that are currently supported by ActionKit.
- [Get Example Payload](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/triggers/api-reference/get-example-payload.md): Get example records for a trigger configuration.
- [List Available Triggers](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/triggers/api-reference/list-available-triggers.md): List all triggers that you can subscribe to on behalf of your user.
- [List Subscribed Triggers](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/triggers/api-reference/list-subscribed-triggers.md): List all triggers that a user is currently subscribed to.
- [Subscribe to a Trigger](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/triggers/api-reference/subscribe-to-trigger.md): Subscribe to a trigger on behalf of your user.
- [Unsubscribe from a Trigger](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/triggers/api-reference/unsubscribe-from-trigger.md): Unsubscribe from a trigger on behalf of your user.
- [Update Subscription](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/triggers/api-reference/update-trigger-subscription.md): Update a trigger subscription's configuration on behalf of your user.
- [Triggers API](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/triggers/overview.md): Subscribe to events from your users' integrations, using our catalog of pre-built triggers or Custom Webhooks.
- [Receiving Webhooks](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/triggers/receiving-webhooks.md): Learn how to receive, validate, and process webhook events for your users' trigger subscriptions.
- [User Configuration](https://docs.useparagon.com/actionkit/triggers/user-configuration.md): Allow users to configure their trigger parameters in your application UI.
- [SDK / API Reference](https://docs.useparagon.com/apis/api-reference.md)
- [JWT Permissions](https://docs.useparagon.com/apis/api-reference/jwt-permissions.md): Use JWT Permissions to control the credentials and configurations that an authenticated user has access to using the Paragon User Token.
- [Multi-Account Authorization](https://docs.useparagon.com/apis/api-reference/multi-account-authorization.md): Use the SDK to connect multiple accounts for the same integration.
- [Multi-Configuration](https://docs.useparagon.com/apis/api-reference/multi-configuration.md): Use the SDK to set up multiple configurations of settings or workflow enablements for one connected account authorization.
- [Proxy API](https://docs.useparagon.com/apis/proxy.md): Send requests directly to an integration provider, on behalf of Connected Users.
- [Task History API](https://docs.useparagon.com/apis/task-history.md)
- [Users API](https://docs.useparagon.com/apis/users.md)
- [Concurrency SLA](https://docs.useparagon.com/billing/concurrency-limits.md): Learn how concurrency is calculated in Paragon.
- [Connected Users](https://docs.useparagon.com/billing/connected-users.md): Learn how Connected Users are counted in Paragon.
- [Task Usage](https://docs.useparagon.com/billing/tasks.md): Learn how tasks are counted in Paragon.
- [Building with Agents](https://docs.useparagon.com/building-with-agents.md): Use Paragon's Agent Skills directly with your agentic IDE of choice
- [API Release Notes](https://docs.useparagon.com/changelog/api.md): Release notes for new versions of the Paragon REST API.
- [Paragraph Release Notes](https://docs.useparagon.com/changelog/paragraph.md): Release notes for new versions of the Paragraph core libraries and CLI.
- [Product Updates](https://docs.useparagon.com/changelog/product-updates.md): Monthly updates on new features and improvements to the Paragon platform.
- [SDK Release Notes](https://docs.useparagon.com/changelog/sdk.md): Release notes for new versions of the Paragon SDK.
- [Customizing the Connect Portal](https://docs.useparagon.com/connect-portal/connect-portal-customization.md): Control your end-user integration experience by customizing Connect Portal.
- [Displaying Workflows](https://docs.useparagon.com/connect-portal/displaying-workflows.md): Control the visibility or customize the description of workflows that appear in the Connect Portal.
- [Field Mapping](https://docs.useparagon.com/connect-portal/field-mapping.md): Use a Field Mapping User Setting to allow your users to define a mapping between objects in your application and their integration.
- [Headless Connect Portal](https://docs.useparagon.com/connect-portal/headless-connect-portal.md): Bring your existing components or design system into your Paragon integrations experience with the Headless Connect Portal.
- [Input Type Reference](https://docs.useparagon.com/connect-portal/input-types-reference.md): Input types to render in the Connect Portal for User Settings.
- [Overview](https://docs.useparagon.com/connect-portal/overview.md): Focus on building out your integration logic and leave integration authentication to Paragon.
- [Workflow Permissions](https://docs.useparagon.com/connect-portal/workflow-permissions.md): Restrict the visibility of workflows to specific users or groups with Workflow Permissions.
- [Custom Dropdowns](https://docs.useparagon.com/connect-portal/workflow-user-settings/custom-dropdowns.md): Provide your users with dropdown options from your application.
- [User Settings](https://docs.useparagon.com/connect-portal/workflow-user-settings/workflow-user-settings.md): Provide settings in the Connect Portal to allow your users to configure their integration and workflows.
- [Hosted Demo Environment](https://docs.useparagon.com/demo.md): Start testing your Paragon integrations without embedding our SDK
- [Working with Multiple Projects](https://docs.useparagon.com/deploying-integrations/projects.md): Create unique spaces for different teams to collaborate.
- [Release Environments](https://docs.useparagon.com/deploying-integrations/release-environments.md): Use Release Environments to test and deploy new integrations and integration updates.
- [Quickstart Sample App](https://docs.useparagon.com/external-link-1.md)
- [Workflow Templates](https://docs.useparagon.com/external-link-4.md)
- [Paragon MCP Server](https://docs.useparagon.com/external-link-5.md)
- [Adding Integrations](https://docs.useparagon.com/getting-started/adding-an-integration.md)
- [Displaying the Connect Portal](https://docs.useparagon.com/getting-started/displaying-the-connect-portal.md): Connect to your users' app accounts with the Connect Portal
- [Installing the SDK](https://docs.useparagon.com/getting-started/installing-the-connect-sdk.md): The Paragon SDK can be imported in your front-end JavaScript to embed the Connect Portal and trigger workflows in your application.
- [ActionKit](https://docs.useparagon.com/learn/actionkit.md): One API. Hundreds of integration actions.
- [Managed Sync](https://docs.useparagon.com/learn/managed-sync.md): Fully managed and normalized 3rd-party data ingestion pipelines surfaced over an easy to use API.
- [Workflows](https://docs.useparagon.com/learn/workflows.md): Embedded iPaaS for building event-driven automations
- [Batch Check Access](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/batch-check-access.md): Check access for a list of users and Synced Objects
- [Check Access](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/check-access.md): Check if a user has access to a specific Synced Object
- [CRM Company](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/company-schema.md): A company, organization, or account in a CRM system.
- [CRM Contact](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/contact-schema.md): A contact record in a CRM system.
- [CRM Custom Object](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/custom-object-schema.md): A custom object record in a CRM system.
- [CRM Deal](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/deal-schema.md): A deal or opportunity record in a CRM system.
- [Delete a Sync](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/delete-sync.md): Delete a Sync for a Connected User, including all associated Synced Objects
- [Disable a Sync](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/disable-sync.md): Disable an active Sync for a Connected User
- [Download File Content](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/download-content.md): Download a file associated with a Synced Object
- [Enable a Sync](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/enable-a-sync.md): Start a Sync for a Connected User with a specific configuration
- [Expand Relationships](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/expand.md): Get all users and group relationships associated with an object by role
- [File](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/file-schema.md): A file stored in a File Storage integration.
- [Get Sync Status](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/get-sync-status.md): Get the current status of a Sync
- [Get Synced Record](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/get-synced-record.md): Get metadata for a specific record in a Sync
- [List Objects](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/list-objects.md): List all accessible objects for a user
- [List Syncs](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/list-syncs.md): List all Syncs for a Connected User
- [List Users](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/list-users.md): List users that have access to a Synced Object by a specific role
- [Pull Synced Records](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/pull-synced-records.md): Get records from a Sync
- [Re-enable a Sync](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/reenable-sync.md): Re-enable an inactive Sync for a Connected User
- [Ticket](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/ticket-schema.md): A ticket's metadata in a Ticketing integration.
- [Update a Sync](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/api/update-sync.md): Update the credential associated with a Sync for a Connected User
- [Getting Started](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/getting-started.md): Try Managed Sync in our Playground environment or using the API.

- [Permissions API](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/permissions-api.md): Learn how to use and implement Permissions API in your app.

- [Webhooks](https://docs.useparagon.com/managed-sync/webhooks.md): Receive webhooks when records are created, updated, or deleted from your Sync.

- [Building a Salesforce Integration](https://docs.useparagon.com/tutorials/building-a-salesforce-integration.md): In this tutorial, we'll build a basic Salesforce integration that syncs leads from your app to your customers' Salesforce accounts.
- [Building a Slack Integration](https://docs.useparagon.com/tutorials/building-a-slack-integration.md): In this tutorial, we'll build a basic Slack integration that sends notifications from your app to your customers' Slack accounts.
- [Building an In-App Integrations Catalog](https://docs.useparagon.com/tutorials/building-an-in-app-integrations-catalog.md): In this tutorial, we'll use the Paragon SDK to build an integrations catalog into your app.
- [Advanced Techniques](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/advanced-techniques.md)
- [Implementing Custom Field Mapping](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/advanced-techniques/implementing-custom-field-mapping.md)
- [Polling Based Workflows](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/advanced-techniques/polling-based-workflows.md)
- [Triggering Workflows in Workflows](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/advanced-techniques/triggering-workflows-in-workflows.md)
- [API Resources](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/api-resources.md): Add API Resources to your project to build reusable Triggers and Steps for your API in Workflows.
- [Auto Retries](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/auto-retries.md): Automatically retry failed step executions.
- [Building Workflows](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/building-workflows.md): Workflows connect data between your users' apps and your application
- [Environment Secrets](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/environment-secrets.md): Securely store and access credentials across your workflows.
- [Using Functions](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/functions.md)
- [Smart Rate Limits](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/handling-rate-limits.md): Learn how Workflows automatically recover from integration API rate limits.
- [Integration Actions](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/integration-actions.md)
- [Workflows](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/overview.md): Workflows allow you to build and automate complex integration logic across your SaaS stack—without managing infrastructure or writing custom code.
- [Making API Requests](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/requests.md): Making HTTP requests with Paragon
- [Request Pagination](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/requests/request-pagination.md): Configuring requests for data that span multiple pages.
- [Triggering Workflows](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/triggers.md): Triggering workflows in Paragon
- [API Resource Trigger](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/triggers/api-resource-trigger.md): Trigger workflows with your existing webhooks API
- [App Events](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/triggers/app-events.md)
- [Integration Enabled Trigger](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/triggers/integration-enabled-trigger.md)
- [Integration Trigger](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/triggers/integration-trigger.md)
- [Request Trigger](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/triggers/request-trigger.md)
- [Scheduler Trigger](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/triggers/scheduler-trigger.md)
- [Using Conditionals](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/using-conditionals.md): Add conditional branching logic to your workflows
- [Using Dynamic Variables](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/using-dynamic-variables.md): Pass data from your application, the Connect Portal, and previous steps.
- [Using Fan Out](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/using-fan-out.md): Run a sequence of steps over each object in an array.
- [Version History](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/version-history.md): Review and restore previous versions of your workflow. Check or revert to earlier workflow versions.
- [Viewing Workflow Executions](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/viewing-workflow-executions.md): Review and debug workflow executions, including step-by-step breakdowns and failure details.
- [Working with Files](https://docs.useparagon.com/workflows/working-with-files.md)
